name: Proportional-Integral (PI) Negative-Feedback Controller
description: A continuous-time ODE-inspired control agent that maintains an internal energy variable via proportional and integral error correction. The agent computes a signed error between its current energy and a set point, then derives a control signal from proportional (instantaneous) and integral (accumulated) components. Actions are selected by evaluating utility functions weighted by this control signal: eating when food is available and drive is positive, moving toward nearest food when hungry, or staying put when satiated. The integral term provides memory of past deficits, enabling persistent foraging even after brief energy upticks, achieving zero steady-state error as in classical integral control. Based on Gross et al. (2024), Drengstig et al. (2012), and the PI glucose homeostasis model from npj Digital Medicine (2020).

variables.name: energy
variables.description: Internal energy level of the agent (the regulated variable). Decreases each tick due to basal metabolism, increases when food is consumed.

variables.name: error_signal
variables.description: Deviation from set point: e(t) = s - A(t). Positive when energy is below target, negative when above.

variables.name: proportional_control
variables.description: Instantaneous corrective drive proportional to the current error. c_P = k_P * e(t).

variables.name: integral_control
variables.description: Accumulated error over time (integral memory). Clamped to prevent windup.

variables.name: total_control_signal
variables.description: Combined drive signal: c(t) = c_P(t) + c_I(t). Drives action selection.

parameters.name: proportional_gain
parameters.source: Tuned; consistent with PI models in npj Digital Medicine (2020)

parameters.name: integral_gain
parameters.source: Tuned; lower than k_P to prevent oscillation (Drengstig et al., 2012)

parameters.name: passive_energy_decay
parameters.source: Models basal metabolic cost; Gross et al. (2024)

parameters.name: energy_from_eating
parameters.source: Tuned to interaction with decay rate d

parameters.name: integral_windup_cap
parameters.source: Standard anti-windup engineering practice; Drengstig et al. (2012)

rules.description: Error signal computation: difference between set point and current energy
Proportional control term: instantaneous corrective drive
Integral control term: accumulated error with anti-windup clamping
Total control signal: sum of proportional and integral components
Energy dynamics: passive decay plus food intake
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if last_action was 'eat' and last_action_result indicates success else 0
A = clamp(A - d + delta_eat * ate, 0, A_max)

rules.description: Eat utility: control signal weighted by food presence at current position
Move utility: control signal weighted by inverse Manhattan distance to nearest food from candidate position
Stay utility: small negative penalty proportional to absolute error

decision_logic.description: PI-controller driven action selection. The agent computes error, updates proportional and integral terms, then selects actions based on utility functions weighted by the total control signal c(t). When error is non-positive (energy >= set point), the agent stays. When error is positive, the agent eats if food is present, moves toward nearest food if visible, or explores randomly.
pseudocode: # Update internal state after receiving perception
ate = 1 if (last_action == 'eat' and last_action_result.get('consumed', False)) else 0
A = clamp(A - d + delta_eat * ate, 0, A_max)
food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list]
return 'stay'  # Energy at or above set point; no foraging drive
min_dist = min(abs(p_prime[0]-fx)+abs(p_prime[1]-fy) for fx,fy in food_positions)
# No food visible: random exploration driven by nonzero control signal

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  — list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.ate_food: last_action == 'eat' and perception['last_action_result'] indicates consumption success

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; agent ignores env reward, uses internal PI control signal as drive

expected_behaviors.description: Energy decays without eating, causing error to grow and control signal to increase
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 20 steps without food available → assert A decreases each step, e increases, c increases

expected_behaviors.description: Agent eats when food is at its position and energy is below set point
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place food at agent position with A < s → assert action == 'eat'

expected_behaviors.description: Agent stays put when energy is at or above set point
Integral term accumulates during prolonged deficit, preventing premature cessation of foraging
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps with A < s → assert c_I > 0 and increasing

expected_behaviors.description: Agent moves toward nearest food when hungry and food is visible
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place food 3 cells to the right, set A < s → assert agent selects 'move_right'

expected_behaviors.description: Agent explores randomly when hungry but no food is visible
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set A < s with no food in resources → assert action is one of move_up/down/left/right

references: Gross, F. et al. (2024) — A Functional Approach to Homeostatic Regulation — Biology Direct
Drengstig, T. et al. (2012) — A Basic Set of Homeostatic Controller Motifs — PMC3491718
npj Digital Medicine (2020) — Homeostasis as a Proportional–Integral Control System — Nature
Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2011) — A Reinforcement Learning Theory for Homeostatic Regulation — NeurIPS
Cannon, W.B. (1932) — The Wisdom of the Body
Bernard, C. (1865) — Introduction à l'étude de la médecine expérimentale
